specific_location: south shore of John Martin Res. near the dam
description: I observed a medium-sized juvenile gull on the south east shore of John Martin Reservoir. The bird was standing in a group of about 25 other gulls that included adult Ring-billed Gulls and adult Franklin's Gulls.  This particular individual was roughly intermediate in size between the RBGUs and the FRGUs The overall plumage was a warm, rich brown with some scaly appearance to the upper parts. The folded primaries were black and extended well beyond the mainly hidden tail. The bird had a prominent blunt black bill and fairly faint white crescent markings by the eye.Behaviors: The bird was standing in a group of other gulls that allowed for size, bill and plumage comparisons.Call: NAPlumage: juvenile
